weakest_eval.justification:
CTAs are elements linking to appropriate URLs, plan cards are
  • items in
      , prices use (not headings). Minor issue: the brand name 'Forge' in the
      is wrapped in a

      rather than a more appropriate element like a or . The

ARIA is used appropriately throughout: aria-current='step' on the active progress item, aria-label on the
, aria-invalid/aria-describedby on the error field, aria-hidden on decorative icons and redundant visible labels. Minor issue: the
carries aria-label='Checkout' when the adjacent

Checkout

could be referenced via aria-labelledby, making the aria-label slightly redundant rather than additive.

Single <h1> ('Running Shoes'), logical h2s for sections ('Showing 6 of 48 results', footer sections, sidebar 'Filters'), and h3 for product names. No skipped levels. A CSS comment explicitly documents that h3 assumes placement under an h2 context and instructs to change it for reuse at the top level, addressing the configurability concern.

All major landmarks are present and correctly labelled (<header>, <nav aria-label='Primary'>, <search>, <aside>, <main>, <footer>, <nav aria-label='Secondary'>), <main> correctly excludes the site header and footer, and three skip links are provided. However, the filter sidebar is missing a <form> wrapper — a key expectation — which means there is no form landmark for the filter controls despite the fieldsets being well-structured internally.

Navigation items are correctly in <ul> within <nav>, the six product cards are wrapped in a <ul> (unordered, as sequence is not meaningful), and footer nav links are in a <ul>. List types are appropriate throughout.

All filter checkboxes have explicit <label> elements, each filter group is wrapped in <fieldset> with <legend>, and the 'in stock only' toggle uses a native checkbox. The search form is properly structured with a <label> and correct input type. The significant gap is the absence of a <form> element wrapping the filter fieldsets, which is a stated key expectation and also means filters cannot be submitted or reset as a unit.

Six distinct, real product names (Nike Air Zoom Pegasus 41, Adidas Ultraboost 24, Brooks Ghost 16, ASICS Gel-Kayano 31, New Balance Fresh Foam X 1080v13, Hoka Clifton 9) with varied, realistic prices ($129.99–$189.95) and product-specific descriptions. Alt text is descriptive and product-specific.
Table uses <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, and all header cells have scope='col'. Dates use <time datetime>. No layout table usage.

expectations_missed: Filter sidebar uses <form> — the fieldsets and labels are correct but there is no wrapping <form> element, so the filters are not a form landmark and cannot be submitted or reset natively
Comment form does not use <fieldset>/<legend> to group the related fields (name, email, comment textarea)
Key expectation listed card names as <h2>, but the output uses <h3> under an <h2> 'All projects' section heading — the h3 is technically more correct given the hierarchy, so this is not a real miss
Yes/No values are conveyed by Unicode symbols (✓/✕) with aria-label overrides rather than visible text ('Yes'/'No'); the expectation calls for visible text to be present alongside or instead of icons
